title: Designed negative control: HLA-B whole-allele dG37 does NOT correlate with mRNA expression (rho ~ +0.017, leave-one-out stable)

subject.display: Migration cohort for 'Designed negative control: HLA-B whole-allele dG37 does NOT correlate with mRNA '
subject.note: Inclusion predicates intentionally empty at migration time; the implicit member set is defined by the v1.1 premises. Re-author with explicit inclusion predicates to tighten the subject.

operations.note: Leave-one-out CV: with n=22, jackknife is essentially LOO. Schema's first-class CVLeaveOneOut variant exercised here.

statistics.name: spearman_rho_full_sample
statistics.note: Near-zero — designed null for HLA-B (contrast with HLA-A rho = -0.71). The 'bulk biophysics' framing fails at HLA-B; the signal lives in 5'UTR-restricted features (separate sibling claims).

statistics.name: max_abs_rho_across_loo_holdouts
statistics.note: Even the most extreme leave-one-out subsample produces |rho| < 0.2 — the null is robust, not driven by hidden cancellation.

inference.justification: Two conjuncts encode the null-as-success register: (1) full-sample |rho| < 0.2 — the bulk effect is small; (2) max LOO |rho| < 0.3 — the null is robust to single-allele removal, ruling out hidden 'rho would be large but for one outlier' patterns. The conclusion's outcome is 'positive' because the designed null IS the expected and informative result: bulk dG37 captures HLA-A but NOT HLA-B, motivating the per-region decomposition that lives in sibling claims (5'UTR-only signal in HLA-B is the actual story).
inference.failure_mode: If s_rho_full exceeded 0.2 OR s_loo_max_abs_rho exceeded 0.3, the bulk-biophysics framing would NOT cleanly separate HLA-A (signal) from HLA-B (null), and the contrast that motivates the regional-decomposition story would weaken.

conclusion.assertion: Across HLA-B alleles in the Bettens 2022 cohort (n=22), whole-allele mean dG37 is essentially uncorrelated with mRNA expression (rho = +0.017), with max LOO |rho| = 0.18. The bulk-biophysics association seen at HLA-A (rho = -0.71) is locus-specific; HLA-B's signal lives in narrower regional decompositions (5'UTR / intron-specific) and is investigated by sibling claims.

scope.scale_note: Whole-allele scope at HLA-B. The HLA-B 5'UTR-specific signal (hla_b_crc_polymorphic_cpg_cluster, etc.) is encoded as separate claims — this null at the bulk scale is consistent with regional structure being load-bearing for HLA-B.

confidence.note: Designed-null register; the success criterion is the absence of a bulk effect, robust under LOO.

external_assumptions.statement: n=22 HLA-B alleles is adequate to detect a |rho| comparable to HLA-A's -0.71 if it existed; failure to detect at this n therefore informatively bounds the bulk effect size.
